message.content: Run CODEX to gate Q6 accessors. Write /tmp/q6a_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 180 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/q6a_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -16' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags). Codex Q6 decision: typed accessors ONLY for proven duplication, byte-identical. Find: {"duplicated_keys":[{"key":"PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL","sites":["src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:663","src/polaris_graph/retrieval/semantic_conflict_detector.py:692"],"computed_default":"os.environ.get(\"PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL\", _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL) — the module-local const _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = \"z-ai/glm-5.2\" is REDEFINED IDENTICALLY in both modules (entailment_judge.py:107 and semantic_conflict_detector.py:80). Real cross-module duplication of both the default value/const and the read. A drift in one const silently diverges the entailment model across the two NLI paths.","note":"cross-module; string read, no coercion"},{"key":"PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES","sites":["src/tools/access_bypass.py:5781","src/tools/access_bypass.py:5950"],"computed_default":"int(os.getenv(\"PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES\", str(5 * 1024 * 1024))) — the exact int()-coerced arithmetic-expression default codex named as the textbook pain point, duplicated verbatim at two call sites (two functions in the same module).","note":"same-module, two functions; int() coercion + str(5*1024*1024) computed default"},{"key":"POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL","sites":["src/polaris_v6/queue/broker.py:76","src/polaris_v6/queue/run_events.py:29"],"computed_default":"os.environ.get(\"POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL\", DEFAULT_REDIS_URL) — the const DEFAULT_REDIS_URL = \"redis://localhost:6379/0\" is REDEFINED IDENTICALLY in both modules (broker.py:22 and run_events.py:24). Real cross-module duplication of the const definition and the read.","note":"cross-module; string read, no coercion"}],"worth_doing":true,"baseline":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors in 37.60s (unchanged from documented phase0/phase1 baseline of 11 pre-existing collection errors). Command: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q. Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase-acc on branch chore/review-readiness-acc (from chore/review-readiness-phase1); phase0 oracle harness (*.py + 3 acceptance cassettes) force-copied in. single_site_keys_count: ~48 — the AST scan of every os.getenv/os.environ.get read with a genuinely computed (non-literal) default found ~51 such keys total; exactly 3 (below) are read at 2+ call sites, the remaining ~48 are read at a single site and are NOT worth an accessor per codex's proven-duplication rule. NOTE: the many int()/float()-coerced keys with a plain STRING-LITERAL default (e.g. int(os.getenv(K,\\\"16384\\\"))) were excluded from the computed-default category: identical-literal ones are already registry-eligible via resolve(), and divergent-literal ones (PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY, PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION, PG_FAITHFULNESS_NLI_THRESHOLD, etc.) are the 20 already-tracked conflicting-default keys in config_conflicts.md, out of scope. Recommendation: a surgical 3-accessor change (typed getters that own each computed default once), NOT a blanket 46-key migration."}. Extract: {"accessors_added":3,"sites_updated":6,"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors","note":"Added 3 typed computed-default accessors to /home/polaris/wt/phase-acc/src/polaris_graph/settings.py: get_entailment_model() -> str (os.environ.get(\"PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL\",\"z-ai/glm-5.2\")), get_max_docling_pdf_bytes() -> int (int(os.getenv(\"PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES\", str(5*1024*1024)))), get_v6_redis_url() -> str (os.environ.get(\"POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL\",\"redis://localhost:6379/0\")). Each does a fresh os.getenv read on every call, preserving env>default precedence and coercion. Replaced 6 call sites: entailment_judge.py:663, semantic_conflict_detector.py:692 (both via `from src.polaris_graph.settings import get_entailment_model`), access_bypass.py:5781 and :5950 (lazy import of get_max_docling_pdf_bytes; PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_PAGES left untouched as a single-site key), broker.py:76 and run_events.py:29 (lazy import of get_v6_redis_url). Byte-identical parity verified with a harness: unset->documented defaults, set->override wins, int() returns int, and a non-int PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES still raises ValueError exactly as the inline int() did (the two access_bypass sites already wrap/guard these). Deliberately did NOT delete the now-orphaned module-local consts: entailment_judge._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL is imported by strict_verify.py and credibility_pass.py and asserted by tests/polaris_graph/test_f3_judge_availability_canary.py and test_entailment_judge_rate_limit_backoff_u16.py; broker.DEFAULT_REDIS_URL is imported+asserted by tests/v6/test_broker.py. Removing them is out of scope for \"replace the call sites\" and would break collection. semantic_conflict_detector._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL and run_events.DEFAULT_REDIS_URL are now unreferenced but left in place (harmless, out of scope). py_compile passed on all 6 touched files; all 6 modules import cleanly. Collection: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (15.27s) — count and the 11 pre-existing error files (mesh/playwright/auth/hle/clarification, none in the modified modules) unchanged from the phase0/phase1 baseline; only wall-clock time differs (non-deterministic)."}. Verify: {"collection_ok":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","characterization_ok":true,"anything_changed":true} (oracle 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98). Ask: "Were accessors added only for genuinely-duplicated computed-defaults (byte-identical, oracle unchanged), OR correctly concluded none warrant one? End ACCESSORS-OK or ACCESSORS-REVISE." Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll run CODEX to gate the Q6 accessors. Let me write the gate file and execute it.

input.content:
You are gating a surgical code change in a large Python codebase (POLARIS). Decide whether typed accessors were added correctly under a strict rule.

## The Rule (Q6 decision)
Typed accessors are warranted ONLY for PROVEN cross-call-site duplication of a *computed* (non-literal) env-var default. The accessor must be byte-identical to the inline reads it replaces (same env-var key, same default value, same coercion, same env>default precedence). Single-site computed-default keys do NOT warrant an accessor. Keys with identical string-literal defaults are already registry-eligible; keys with divergent literal defaults are tracked conflicts and out of scope.

## What was found (AST scan)
~51 keys read with a genuinely computed (non-literal) default. Exactly 3 are read at 2+ call sites; the other ~48 are single-site (NOT worth an accessor). The 3 duplicated keys:

1. PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL — read at entailment_judge.py:663 and semantic_conflict_detector.py:692. Default via module-local const _DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2", REDEFINED IDENTICALLY in both modules (entailment_judge.py:107, semantic_conflict_detector.py:80). Cross-module dup of const + read. String read, no coercion.

2. PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES — read at access_bypass.py:5781 and :5950 (two functions, same module). Default int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES", str(5*1024*1024))). int() coercion + computed default, duplicated verbatim.

3. POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL — read at broker.py:76 and run_events.py:29. Default via const DEFAULT_REDIS_URL = "redis://localhost:6379/0", REDEFINED IDENTICALLY in both modules (broker.py:22, run_events.py:24). Cross-module dup. String read, no coercion.

## What was done (extract)
Added 3 typed accessors to src/polaris_graph/settings.py:
- get_entailment_model() -> str: os.environ.get("PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL","z-ai/glm-5.2")
- get_max_docling_pdf_bytes() -> int: int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES", str(5*1024*1024)))
- get_v6_redis_url() -> str: os.environ.get("POLARIS_V6_REDIS_URL","redis://localhost:6379/0")

Each does a fresh os.getenv read per call, preserving env>default precedence and coercion. Replaced all 6 call sites (entailment_judge.py:663, semantic_conflict_detector.py:692, access_bypass.py:5781 & :5950, broker.py:76, run_events.py:29).

Deliberately did NOT delete now-orphaned module-local consts:
- entailment_judge._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL: imported by strict_verify.py + credibility_pass.py, asserted by 2 tests -> removing breaks collection.
- broker.DEFAULT_REDIS_URL: imported+asserted by tests/v6/test_broker.py.
- semantic_conflict_detector._DEFAULT_ENTAILMENT_MODEL and run_events.DEFAULT_REDIS_URL now unreferenced but left in place (harmless, out of scope).

Byte-identical parity verified with a harness: unset->documented defaults, set->override wins, int() returns int, non-int PG_MAX_DOCLING_PDF_BYTES still raises ValueError exactly as inline int() did. py_compile passed on all 6 files; all 6 modules import cleanly.

## Verify (independent)
collection_ok: true (16738 tests collected, 11 errors — unchanged from phase0/phase1 baseline of 11 pre-existing collection errors, none in modified modules)
oracle_matches: true, oracle_sha 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98
characterization_ok: true
anything_changed: true

## Your decision
Were accessors added ONLY for genuinely-duplicated computed-defaults (byte-identical, oracle unchanged), OR was it correctly concluded none warrant one? Consider: (a) is the 3-vs-48 split defensible? (b) are all 3 truly byte-identical replacements? (c) does leaving orphaned consts in place create a NEW drift risk that defeats the dedup purpose? (d) any single-site key wrongly given an accessor, or any true duplicate missed?

End your answer with exactly one line: either "ACCESSORS-OK" or "ACCESSORS-REVISE".

input.description: Run codex gate on Q6 accessors

content.content:
**Assessing unreferenced constant cleanup impact**
**Validating environment accessor equivalence**
**Confirming accessor preservation despite orphan constants**
codex
The 3-vs-48 split is defensible: only the three keys have multiple computed-default read sites. All six replacements preserve the exact key, default, coercion, fresh-read behavior, and env-over-default precedence.

Leaving compatibility constants does not alter accessor behavior or the oracle. They remain a maintenance risk, but not a correctness failure; they can later be aliased to canonical settings constants. No single-site key received an accessor, and no identified duplicate was missed.

ACCESSORS-OK
